name: pixiewps

documentation.readme: Pixiewps is a tool written in C used to bruteforce offline the WPS PIN exploiting the low or non-existing entropy of some software implementations, the so-called "pixie-dust attack" discovered by Dominique Bongard in summer 2014. It is meant for educational purposes only.
usage_sections: Versions 1.4 and later make use of multi-threading and require libpthread (POSIX threads)
The data in input can be formatted with one of the following byte separators: ':', '-', ' ', or without

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: : Verbose help and more usage examples

arguments.name: --jobs
arguments.description: : Number of parallel threads to use [Auto]

arguments.name: --m5-enc
arguments.description: : Recover secret nonce from M5 (only mode 3)

arguments.name: --m7-enc
arguments.description: : Recover encrypted settings from M7 (only mode 3)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd pixiewps*/`. Further invocations from upstream: `Usage: pixiewps <arguments>`; `pixiewps --pke ... --pkr ... --e-hash1 ... --e-hash2 ... --authkey ... --e-nonce ...`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: Overview [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-GPL%20v3%2B-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](LICENSE.md).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Pixiewps is a tool written in C used to bruteforce offline the WPS PIN exploiting the low or non-existing entropy of some software implementations, the so-called "pixie-dust attack" discovered by Dominique Bongard in summer 2014. It is meant for educational purposes only.
